raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes revisiting the framing of selective prediction, calibration, and the contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft's argument from calibration measurements to selective-prediction and deployment recommendations requires checking the premise-to-conclusion chain.","R1.3":"The empirical ECE results are used to support broad claims about model trust and safety, so claim-evidence alignment is central.","R1.5":"Improving the draft can reasonably include section-level narrative changes to how the object of study, methods, results, and recommendations are ordered.","R1.6":"A request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision load-bearing.","R1.7":"The draft invokes prior work and metric conventions, so improving it includes checking attribution, reference accuracy, and literature coverage.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of improvement is intended and about claims not verifiable from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong deployment and sufficiency claims, making avoidance of overclaiming an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not actually improving or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the draft discusses safety-critical deployment, projected responsibilities must account for avoiding downstream harm from unsupported recommendations.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes revisiting the framing of selective prediction, calibration, and the contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft's argument from calibration measurements to selective-prediction and deployment recommendations requires checking the premise-to-conclusion chain.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical ECE results are used to support broad claims about model trust and safety, so claim-evidence alignment is central.
rationale.R1.5: Improving the draft can reasonably include section-level narrative changes to how the object of study, methods, results, and recommendations are ordered.
rationale.R1.6: A request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision load-bearing.
rationale.R1.7: The draft invokes prior work and metric conventions, so improving it includes checking attribution, reference accuracy, and literature coverage.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of improvement is intended and about claims not verifiable from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong deployment and sufficiency claims, making avoidance of overclaiming an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not actually improving or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft discusses safety-critical deployment, projected responsibilities must account for avoiding downstream harm from unsupported recommendations.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the provided artifact.
